description: Remove `progress.txt` and the old implementation `tasks.json` from the branch before merging. These are developer tracking artifacts that should not be committed to the repository. Acceptance: neither `progress.txt` nor `tasks.json` appear in the final commit diff against main (after QA tasks are moved or tracked elsewhere).
steps-to-verify: Run: `git diff --stat main..HEAD` and confirm progress.txt is absent
Confirm tasks.json is either removed or replaced with QA-only tasks

description: Add a `### ddl provision` section to the SchemaTools README.md documenting the new subcommand. It should mirror the style of the existing `### ddl emit` section and include: command synopsis, options table (`--schema`, `--connection-string`, `--dialect`, `--create-database`), usage examples for both pgsql and mssql dialects, and a note that `--dialect both` is not accepted. Acceptance: `README.md` documents all four options with descriptions, defaults, and at least two usage examples.
steps-to-verify: Confirm a `ddl provision` section exists with options table and examples
Confirm --dialect documents that only pgsql/mssql are accepted (not both)

description: In `MssqlDatabaseProvisioner.CheckOrConfigureMvcc`, the `quotedName` variable (line 131) is computed unconditionally but is only used in the `databaseWasCreated == true` branch. Move the `quotedName` computation inside the `if (databaseWasCreated)` block so it is not allocated in the check-only path. Acceptance: `quotedName` is only computed when `databaseWasCreated` is true; all existing unit tests still pass; build succeeds with zero warnings.
steps-to-verify: Open MssqlDatabaseProvisioner.cs and confirm quotedName is inside the if-block

description: In both `PgsqlDatabaseProvisioner.CreateDatabaseIfNotExists` (line 28-29) and `MssqlDatabaseProvisioner.CreateDatabaseIfNotExists` (line 30-31), the connection string builder is constructed on one line and then `GetDatabaseName()` is called on the next line, which internally constructs a second builder from the same connection string. Eliminate the redundant parse by extracting the database name directly from the first builder instance. For PgsqlDatabaseProvisioner: use `builder.Database` with the same null/empty check. For MssqlDatabaseProvisioner: use `builder.InitialCatalog` with the same null/empty check. Acceptance: each `CreateDatabaseIfNotExists` method constructs only one connection string builder; behavior is identical; all tests pass.

The static methods `CreateDialect()` and `CloneEffectiveSchemaSet()` are identical copies in both `DdlEmitCommand.cs` (lines 230-265) and `DdlProvisionCommand.cs` (lines 191-236). Extract these into a shared static helper class (e.g., `DdlCommandHelpers` in the Commands namespace) and have both commands call the shared versions. Acceptance: `CreateDialect` and `CloneEffectiveSchemaSet` exist in exactly one location; both commands reference the shared helper; all 34 tests pass; build succeeds.
steps-to-verify: Confirm CreateDialect() and CloneEffectiveSchemaSet() exist in only one shared location
Confirm DdlEmitCommand and DdlProvisionCommand both reference the shared helpers

description: In `MssqlDatabaseProvisioner.CheckOrConfigureMvcc` (line 170), the RCSI check uses `result is bool rcsiEnabled && !rcsiEnabled` to match the `ExecuteScalar` result. SQL Server's `sys.databases.is_read_committed_snapshot_on` is a `bit` column, and ADO.NET maps it to `bool`. However, if the database no longer exists between the DDL step and the MVCC check, `ExecuteScalar` returns `null`, and the `is bool` pattern silently skips the warning — potentially masking a serious problem. Add an explicit null check before the pattern match: if `result` is null, log an error indicating the database was not found in `sys.databases`. Acceptance: when `ExecuteScalar` returns null, a clear error message is logged; the existing bool-true and bool-false paths are unchanged; all tests pass.
steps-to-verify: Confirm the null case logs an error or warning about database not found in sys.databases

description: The `DatabaseProvisionerTests` test fixture class declares `[TestFixture]` on the outer class but has no test methods of its own. The inner classes inherit from it (`: DatabaseProvisionerTests`) but the outer class provides no shared members, setup, or teardown. Remove the `[TestFixture]` attribute from the outer class and remove the `: DatabaseProvisionerTests` inheritance from all inner classes, since they serve no purpose. Acceptance: the outer class has no `[TestFixture]` attribute; inner classes do not inherit from it; all 9 tests still pass.
steps-to-verify: Confirm outer DatabaseProvisionerTests class has no [TestFixture] attribute
Confirm inner test fixture classes do not inherit from DatabaseProvisionerTests
